utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: tyler nelson , date of birth : 3 , 6 , 1949 , mrn , 385729 . he is here today for ongoing management of rheumatoid arthritis . last time he was here , he received a steroid injection in his right knee . hello mr. nelson , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , about the same as last time . my knee is still swollen .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that was your right knee , correct ? i take it the injection did n't help ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it did , maybe for a couple of weeks .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's it ? and is it still painful ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it's pretty painful , and now the whole leg is getting swollen .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , what would you say your pain level is ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i mean , right now it's about a 3 or so , but , typically on a normal day it's around 6 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and its been swollen like that for how long now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: since i was last here a couple weeks after that . so about , maybe two months now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , but before it was just the knee ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , the whole thing was n't swollen like this , but i tried to wear compression socks a lot of the time , but it has n't been helping .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , okay , so that has n't been working for you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i mean , it helps to some degree , but it's still swollen , especially around the knee .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmm . has it been keeping you from doing things during the day ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i slowed down a little bit , and i found a few things too fast . but when it really hurts , is when i've been sitting for a while , and then i have to try and get up . i'm so stiff getting up i have to use a cane to get my bearings . i mean , after a little bit , it loosens up , but that first getting up is real difficult .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i can see , that makes sense . have you been taking your medication regularly ? your on xeljanz right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , the xr , i think its , uhm , 11 milligrams a day ? yeah , but i'm taking it everyday , and i'm also taking celebrex which i started maybe 2 months ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes , the 200mgs daily . how's that working for you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: pretty good , up until it started swelling again .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , well come sit over here , and if you could take of your socks and shoes , and we'll take a look at your knee . and just to let you know , i have a service now that writes my note about the visit , so you'll hear me call things out and describe what i see fully .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , gotcha .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: right , so let's check your leg . can you straighten it , and does it hurt ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , when i try to straighten it all the way it does .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , range of motion slightly diminished . i'm going to press here , okay ? and how about here , hmm , i see some pitting around the knee and ankle . normal capillary refill in the toes . does any of this area hurt ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , just kinda sore , like an old bruise ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , i can feel that the knee is hot , and that means there's something going on , like some inflammation . so we can give you another steroid injection for that . i also want to get an x-ray of that knee , so we can see where all the swelling is coming from .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . let's do it . sounds go to me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and did we set up physical therapy for you last time you were here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that was some years ago , but that was the other knee .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so i think we'll set that up for you now . we'll also do an x-ray on that knee . i can send you for a physical therapy , and if nothing is helping , then we really might need to get a surgery consult to see what we can do with that knee .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , sounds like a plan .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , so for today we'll go ahead and we'll give you the steroid shot . i'll try to see if i can get a little bit of fluid out before i do give you the injection . there might be no fluid , but i'll see if i can get anything out regardless .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . and that's a procedure you're going to do right now ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yep . once we're done talking here , and as far as the edema in your legs , uh , we'll take an x-ray to look for the cause , but there is a lot you can do as well . the compression socks are a good start . um , and also , do you eat a lot of salt ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i mean , um , i mean i love my salty snacks .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well , salt does have a tendency to make your body hold on to water , reducing your salt intake could help with some of the swelling . you can also prop your feet up any time you get a chance , especially at night . and have you talked to your private care doctor about this at all ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i figured i would just come here first .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so i think , try some of those things out first , and if it does n't help , definitely make an appointment with them , also , as you know , xeljanz is a high-risk medication , so we need to check your cmp levels every couple of months . so this time your levels are good , but do make sure to make an appointment for the blood work for next time .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right . i can do that . thank you so much doc .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes , of course . well let me go ahead and get supplies and we'll get to work on your knee .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: fluid aspiration and steroid injection procedure . the risks , benefits , and alternatives were explained . the risks of steroid injection were explained , including , but not limited to : hypopigmentation , soft tissue atrophy , tendon ruptures , and transient hypoglycemia among others . verbal consent was attained . the right knee was , uh , um , accessed through the lateral approach with 3 milliliters of initially clear fluid , then bloo- blood tinged fluid . then the right knee was prepped in a standard-fashion . the right knee was injected with 80 milligrams of triamcinolone , and one milliliter of lidocaine . the procedure was tolerated well , with no complications . post-procedure instructions were given . patient will follow up with his pcp . thank you .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 72-year-old male presenting for rheumatoid arthritis management.
Rheumatoid arthritis management.
The patient reports persistent right knee swelling.
The doctor asks if the previous steroid injection was ineffective.
The patient reports the previous steroid injection provided relief for only a few weeks.
The doctor asks if the patient is still experiencing pain.
The patient reports persistent pain and new swelling in the entire right leg.
The doctor asks the patient to rate their pain level.
The patient reports a typical pain level of 6/10.
The doctor asks about the duration of the leg swelling.
The patient reports the leg swelling has been present for approximately two months.
The patient reports using compression socks, which provide limited relief.
The patient reports stiffness when standing after sitting, requiring a cane for support.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking their medication regularly.
The patient is taking Xeljanz XR 11 mg daily and Celebrex 200 mg daily.
The doctor instructs the patient to prepare for a physical examination of the knee.
The doctor asks the patient to straighten their leg to assess for pain.
The patient reports pain when fully straightening the right leg.
Physical exam findings include reduced range of motion, pitting edema around the knee and ankle, and normal capillary refill.
The doctor notes the knee is warm, indicating inflammation, and plans a steroid injection and knee x-ray.
The doctor asks if physical therapy was previously arranged.
The doctor plans to order a knee x-ray, refer the patient to physical therapy, and mentions a potential surgery consult if symptoms persist.
The doctor plans to perform fluid aspiration and a steroid injection.
The doctor asks if the patient consumes a high-salt diet.
The doctor advises reducing salt intake, elevating feet, and consulting the PCP regarding edema.
The doctor asks if the patient has discussed the edema with their PCP.
The doctor instructs the patient to schedule blood work for CMP monitoring due to high-risk medication use.
The procedure included fluid aspiration and injection of 80 mg triamcinolone and 1 mL lidocaine into the right knee.
